questions.promptText: She has been living in Mumbai ____ 2015.
questions.explanation: 'Since' is used with a specific point in time; 'for' would be used with a duration.

questions.promptText: He is good ____ mathematics.
questions.explanation: 'Good at' is the standard idiomatic preposition.

questions.promptText: Neither the manager nor the employees ____ aware of the new policy.
questions.explanation: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject ('employees', plural).

questions.promptText: The train arrived ____ time despite the delay.
questions.explanation: 'On time' means punctual/as scheduled.

questions.promptText: She insisted ____ paying the bill.
questions.explanation: 'Insist on' is the correct idiomatic preposition.

questions.promptText: He is allergic ____ peanuts.
questions.explanation: 'Allergic to' is the fixed preposition.

questions.promptText: They congratulated him ____ his success.
questions.explanation: 'Congratulate someone on something' is the standard usage.

questions.promptText: The committee ____ yet to decide on the matter.
questions.explanation: 'Committee' is treated as a singular collective noun here.

questions.promptText: She apologized ____ being late.
questions.explanation: 'Apologize for' is the correct preposition.

questions.promptText: The company's profits ____ significantly last quarter.
questions.explanation: Context (no contradiction implied) supports a straightforward positive change.

questions.promptText: His speech was so ____ that half the audience fell asleep.
questions.explanation: The audience falling asleep implies a dull, monotonous speech.

questions.promptText: The scientist's theory was later ____ by new evidence.
questions.explanation: 'Later' + context of new evidence suggests the theory was shown false.

questions.promptText: Despite the criticism, she remained ____ in her decision.
questions.explanation: 'Despite criticism' + 'remained' implies she stayed firm.

questions.promptText: The negotiations ended in a ____ after both sides refused to compromise.
questions.explanation: Refusing to compromise leads to a stalemate, i.e. deadlock.

questions.promptText: His explanation was ____ and left no room for doubt.
questions.explanation: 'Left no room for doubt' implies clarity, i.e. lucid.

questions.promptText: The team of researchers (a)/ have completed (b)/ their study (c)/ successfully. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Team' is a singular collective noun here, so it should be 'has completed'.

questions.promptText: Each of the students (a)/ have submitted (b)/ their assignments (c)/ on time. (d)
questions.explanation: 'Each' takes a singular verb: 'has submitted'.

questions.promptText: She don't like (a)/ to travel alone (b)/ especially at night. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: Third person singular subject 'She' requires 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.promptText: One of the boys (a)/ were late (b)/ for the morning assembly. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'One of the boys' is singular, so it should be 'was late'.

questions.promptText: The news of his resignation (a)/ were shocking (b)/ to everyone in the office. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'News' is treated as singular, so 'was shocking' is correct.

questions.promptText: Neither of the answers (a)/ are correct (b)/ according to the answer key. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Neither' takes a singular verb: 'is correct'.

questions.promptText: He is one of the best player (a)/ in the team (b)/ this season. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'One of the best' must be followed by a plural noun: 'players'.

questions.promptText: The number of accidents (a)/ have increased (b)/ this year. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'The number of' takes a singular verb: 'has increased'.

questions.promptText: She is more taller (a)/ than her brother (b)/ by two inches. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Taller' is already comparative; 'more taller' is a double comparative error.

questions.promptText: I have been knowing him (a)/ since we were (b)/ in school together. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb and isn't used in continuous form; it should be 'have known'.

questions.promptText: Everyone of the participants (a)/ were given (b)/ a certificate. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Everyone' is singular, so it should be 'was given'.

questions.promptText: Between you and I (a)/ this plan (b)/ will not work. (c)/ No error (d)
questions.explanation: 'Between' is a preposition and takes the objective case: 'you and me'.

questions.promptText: The manager along with his employees are attending the seminar.
questions.explanation: 'Along with' doesn't make the subject plural; the verb agrees with 'manager' (singular).

questions.promptText: If I was you, I would accept the offer.
questions.explanation: Hypothetical/subjunctive mood requires 'were', regardless of subject.

questions.promptText: She is one of the employee who always arrive on time.
questions.explanation: 'One of the' requires a plural noun, and the relative clause verb agrees with that plural noun.

questions.promptText: Not only he is talented but also hardworking.
questions.explanation: 'Not only' at the start of a clause requires subject-verb inversion.

questions.promptText: By the time we reach the station, the train will leave.
questions.explanation: Future perfect tense is needed for an action completed before another future action.

questions.promptText: He suggested me to consult a doctor.
questions.explanation: 'Suggest' is not followed by 'object + to-infinitive'; it takes a 'that'-clause.

questions.promptText: The reason he was late is because he missed the bus.
questions.explanation: 'The reason is that...' is the grammatically correct construction; 'reason is because' is redundant.

questions.promptText: Scarcely had he left the office when it started raining.
questions.explanation: This sentence correctly uses 'scarcely...had...when' with inversion.

questions.promptText: Each of the boys have their own opinion.
questions.explanation: 'Each' is singular, requiring 'has', and traditionally paired with a singular pronoun 'his'.

questions.promptText:
P: Finally, the results were released and celebrated across the campus.
Q: The university announced a new exam pattern last year.
R: Students initially resisted the change, fearing lower scores.
S: Over time, however, most adapted well to the new format.
questions.explanation: Logical flow: announcement (Q) -> resistance (R) -> adaptation (S) -> results celebrated (P).

questions.promptText:
P: This led to a significant drop in production costs.
Q: The factory switched to automated machinery in 2020.
R: As a result, profits rose sharply within a year.
S: Workers were retrained to operate the new systems.
questions.explanation: Switch to automation (Q) -> retraining (S) -> cost drop (P) -> profit rise (R).

questions.promptText:
P: Despite the odds, the team fought back valiantly.
Q: The match started with the home team taking an early lead.
R: Their opponents responded with two quick goals before halftime.
S: In the end, the game ended in a thrilling draw.
questions.explanation: Early lead (Q) -> opponents equalize (R) -> fightback (P) -> draw (S).

questions.promptText:
P: Consequently, several small businesses had to shut down.
Q: The new tax policy was introduced amid much debate.
R: It significantly raised operational costs for small firms.
S: The government later revised the policy after public outcry.
questions.explanation: Policy introduced (Q) -> raised costs (R) -> shutdowns (P) -> revision (S).

questions.promptText:
P: This made the region highly vulnerable to landslides.
Q: Heavy deforestation occurred in the hilly areas over decades.
R: The soil lost its ability to hold water during monsoons.
S: Authorities have since launched a large-scale afforestation drive.
questions.explanation: Deforestation (Q) -> soil degradation (R) -> vulnerability (P) -> afforestation response (S).

questions.promptText:
P: The plan was abandoned after initial trials failed.
Q: Engineers proposed building a bridge across the river.
R: A cheaper, tunnel-based alternative was then suggested.
S: This alternative was accepted and completed successfully.
questions.explanation: Proposal (Q) -> failed trials (P) -> alternative suggested (R) -> completed (S).

questions.promptText:
P: Sales, however, remained flat despite the campaign.
Q: The company launched an aggressive marketing campaign.
R: Analysts later found the ads targeted the wrong audience.
S: A revised campaign was launched with better targeting.
questions.explanation: Campaign launched (Q) -> flat sales (P) -> cause found (R) -> revised campaign (S).

questions.promptText:
P: As a result, the flight was delayed by three hours.
Q: Heavy fog covered the airport early in the morning.
R: Visibility dropped below the minimum safe level for landing.
S: Passengers were informed through the airline's app.
questions.explanation: Fog (Q) -> low visibility (R) -> delay (P) -> passengers informed (S).

questions.promptText:
P: The village finally got access to clean drinking water.
Q: An NGO surveyed the village's water sources last year.
R: They found the wells contaminated with industrial waste.
S: A new filtration plant was installed within six months.
questions.explanation: Survey (Q) -> contamination found (R) -> plant installed (S) -> clean water access (P).

questions.promptText: A person who can speak many languages
questions.explanation: A 'polyglot' is someone fluent in several languages.

questions.promptText: A place where birds are kept
questions.explanation: An 'aviary' is a large enclosure specifically for birds.

questions.promptText: One who is not easily discouraged
questions.explanation: 'Indefatigable' describes someone who is tireless and persistent.

questions.promptText: A statement that contradicts itself
questions.explanation: A 'paradox' is a statement that seems self-contradictory but may hold truth.

questions.promptText: One who loves mankind
questions.explanation: A 'philanthropist' is someone devoted to promoting the welfare of others.

questions.promptText: Government by the few
questions.explanation: 'Oligarchy' specifically means rule by a small group of people.

questions.promptText:
Solar power has become one of the fastest-growing sources of electricity worldwide. Falling panel costs, government subsidies, and rising public awareness about climate change have all contributed to this growth. However, solar energy still faces challenges: it cannot be generated at night, and storing excess energy for later use remains expensive. Many countries are now investing in battery storage technology to address this limitation, aiming to make solar power a reliable, round-the-clock energy source.

According to the passage, why has solar power grown rapidly?

options.text: Falling costs, subsidies, and climate awareness

questions.explanation: The passage directly attributes growth to falling costs, subsidies, and climate awareness.

questions.promptText:
Solar power has become one of the fastest-growing sources of electricity worldwide. Falling panel costs, government subsidies, and rising public awareness about climate change have all contributed to this growth. However, solar energy still faces challenges: it cannot be generated at night, and storing excess energy for later use remains expensive. Many countries are now investing in battery storage technology to address this limitation, aiming to make solar power a reliable, round-the-clock energy source.

What is identified as a major limitation of solar energy?

options.text: Inability to generate power at night and expensive storage

questions.explanation: The passage states solar can't be generated at night and storage is expensive.

questions.promptText:

However, solar energy still faces challenges: it cannot be generated at night, and storing excess energy for later use remains expensive. Many countries are now investing in battery storage technology to address this limitation, aiming to make solar power a reliable, round-the-clock energy source.

What are countries doing to address this limitation?
questions.explanation: The passage says countries are investing in battery storage technology.

questions.promptText:
Urban migration has reshaped cities across the developing world. As people move from rural areas in search of better jobs and education, cities expand rapidly, often outpacing infrastructure development. This creates strain on housing, transportation, and sanitation systems. While migration boosts urban economies through cheap labor, it also widens the gap between planned growth and actual population needs, leading to the rise of informal settlements in many major cities.

Why do people migrate to urban areas, according to the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage states people move in search of better jobs and education.

questions.promptText:
Urban migration has reshaped cities across the developing world. As people move from rural areas in search of better jobs and education, cities expand rapidly, often outpacing infrastructure development. This creates strain on housing, transportation, and sanitation systems. While migration boosts urban economies through cheap labor, it also widens the gap between planned growth and actual population needs, leading to the rise of informal settlements in many major cities.

What problem does rapid urban migration create?

options.text: Infrastructure struggling to keep pace with population growth

questions.explanation: The passage says cities expand faster than infrastructure development.

questions.promptText:
Urban migration has reshaped cities across the developing world. As people move from rural areas in search of better jobs and education, cities expand rapidly, often outpacing infrastructure development. This creates strain on housing, transportation, and sanitation systems. While migration boosts urban economies through cheap labor, it also widens the gap between planned growth and actual population needs, leading to the rise of informal settlements in many major cities.

What is mentioned as a consequence of the gap between planned growth and population needs?
questions.explanation: The passage directly states this gap leads to informal settlements.

questions.promptText:
A recent workplace study found that employees who took short breaks every 90 minutes reported higher levels of sustained focus than those who worked continuously for long stretches. The study attributed this to the brain's limited capacity for uninterrupted attention, which tends to decline sharply after prolonged periods without rest. Companies that implemented structured break policies saw a measurable improvement in overall productivity, even though total working hours were reduced.

What did the study find about employees taking regular short breaks?
questions.explanation: The passage states employees taking breaks reported higher sustained focus.

questions.promptText:
A recent workplace study found that employees who took short breaks every 90 minutes reported higher levels of sustained focus than those who worked continuously for long stretches. The study attributed this to the brain's limited capacity for uninterrupted attention, which tends to decline sharply after prolonged periods without rest. Companies that implemented structured break policies saw a measurable improvement in overall productivity, even though total working hours were reduced.

Why does the brain's attention decline during long uninterrupted work periods?

options.text: Due to limited capacity for sustained attention

questions.explanation: The passage attributes this to the brain's limited capacity for uninterrupted attention.

questions.promptText:
A recent workplace study found that employees who took short breaks every 90 minutes reported higher levels of sustained focus than those who worked continuously for long stretches. The study attributed this to the brain's limited capacity for uninterrupted attention, which tends to decline sharply after prolonged periods without rest. Companies that implemented structured break policies saw a measurable improvement in overall productivity, even though total working hours were reduced.

options.text: Improved productivity despite fewer working hours
